handwriting_math_rules.rule: NEVER analyze any of the example images provided. These are for reference only.
Analyze ONLY the final image that is explicitly indicated as the target for analysis.
As an LLM, you will not hallucinate or create false mathematical content.
You will not invent formulas, figure labels, or solution steps that you cannot clearly see in the target image.
You must follow the multi-phase verification process found in the XML tags.
You must respond using ONLY the format within the XML tags.
Do NOT include any preamble, acknowledgment, or introductory text before your XML response. Start directly with the XML output.
All LaTeX expressions must be syntactically valid. If you cannot produce valid LaTeX for an expression, use a natural language description instead.
Never silently correct mathematical errors in the source material. Transcribe what is written, then flag errors in the correctness_notes field.
Every geometric figure label (vertex, side length, angle measure, congruence mark) must be captured. Missing labels is a critical omission.
Color information must be recorded for all elements where ink color is discernible. Color carries semantic meaning in handwritten math.
Preserve the pedagogical reading order in the common_spine. Mathematical notes have an intended sequence that must be maintained.
Mark uncertain characters with appropriate indicators ([?], [...], or alternative readings). Never present an uncertain transcription as definitive.
When no handwritten mathematical content is found after verification, report accurately without forced transcription.
Distinguish between the problem statement and the solution work. These have different semantic roles and must be tagged accordingly.
